question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getRetry() / RetryProperties.getMinBackoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set retry.minBackoff to exactly 100ms (the @DurationMin boundary) and retry.maxAttempts to Integer.MAX_VALUE, creating a scenario where the @Retryable annotation retries at 100ms intervals indefinitely, consuming a thread permanently and starving the synchronized parse() method from processing new record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: @ConfigurationProperties binding] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Unicode or special-character string into a Duration-typed property (e.g., frequency=PT\u00001S) to exploit a parsing edge case in Spring's DurationConverter, causing an unhandled exception during property binding that crashes the application context?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user who can observe the health endpoint determine whether the record parser is enabled or disabled via the RecordStreamFileHealthIndicator, and use this information to time attacks against the mirror node when parsing is known to be disabled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getBatch() / BatchProperties.getWindow()] Can an unprivileged external user set batch.window to a Duration larger than the record file close interval (2 seconds for StreamType.RECORD), causing the batch window to span multiple record files and creating a scenario where a failure mid-window causes multiple record files' data to be rolled back together, amplifying data loss?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getRetry() / RetryProperties] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that OutOfMemoryError is the only excluded exception in @Retryable (excludes = OutOfMemoryError.class), meaning all other errors including StackOverflowError, ThreadDeath, and other Errors are retried indefinitely, causing the retry loop to consume resources even on unrecoverable JVM errors?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getFrequency() / getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to 10ms and batch.queueCapacity to 1, creating a scenario where the producer fills the queue in 10ms but the consumer cannot drain it fast enough, causing the producer thread to block indefinitely and halting the record file download pipeline?
